title: Dubai Autism Center launches region’s first adaptive "dynamic" education model | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: The goal is to create an atmosphere that is both safe and stimulating, enabling every student to reach their full potential.
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
UAE—The Dubai Autism Center (DAC) has become the first specialized institution in the Middle East to adopt what it calls a “dynamic approach” across its educational and rehabilitation services.

The move marks a significant departure from conventional autism support models and signals a broader ambition to make Dubai a regional leader in the care and development of People of Determination.

Rather than relying on static, one-size-fits-all support systems, the Center’s new framework treats the educational environment as a living, responsive system—one that continuously adapts to each student’s sensory, behavioral, and emotional state.

The goal is to create an atmosphere that is both safe and stimulating, enabling every student to reach their full potential.

From formal accessibility to comprehensive adaptation

Mohammed Al Emadi, Director General and Board Member of the Dubai Autism Center, described the shift as a strategic commitment rooted in global best practices.

He explained that empowering students with autism requires more than traditional, fixed services — it demands a flexible educational ecosystem that can respond to students’ ever-changing needs.

Al Emadi also positioned the initiative as part of a broader effort to establish Dubai as a regional hub for comprehensive rehabilitation.

In practical terms, the Center continuously adjusts educational and behavioral plans, reconfigures classrooms to accommodate individual sensory sensitivities, and provides specialized facilities including sensory integration rooms, life-skills training programs, and speech therapy sessions.

All of these services are delivered within learning spaces designed to encourage growth through interaction and play.

Evidence-based methods at the core

On the clinical side, Dr. Nicholas Orland, Chief Program Officer at the Dubai Autism Center, pointed to the model’s grounding in Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) as a key strength.

The approach integrates advanced ABA methodologies with flexible tools that allow clinicians to design highly individualized interventions for each student.

To ensure quality, the Center’s team has undergone extensive training focused on consistent and faithful implementation of the model.

Dr. Orland noted that the results are already visible in students’ progress across communication, independence, and social interaction.

By providing an environment that evolves alongside each child’s needs, the Center aims to strengthen self-confidence and help students express abilities they might not otherwise have the opportunity to demonstrate.

Three pillars of the dynamic approach

The Center has built its dynamic model around three core features.

The first is Dynamic Sensory Regulation Rooms, which allow staff to immediately adjust lighting and sound levels in response to a child’s real-time reactions.

The second is flexible classrooms, which are designed to either minimize distractions or increase interactive stimuli depending on each student’s Individualized Education Plan (IEP).

The third is Life Simulation Spaces, which focus on building daily independence and flexible thinking to prepare students for effective participation in the wider community.

Current reach

The Dubai Autism Center currently serves 159 children with autism.

Of these, 97 are enrolled in the integrated school system, while 62 receive specialized individual clinical services.

The adoption of the dynamic approach is expected to improve learning outcomes and social integration for all students under the Center’s care, equipping them with skills that support long-term success in society.
